content.problemStatement:
Given a sorted array of integers AAA and a target integer XXX. Write a program to insert the target integer into the array such that the array remains sorted after the insertion.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer NNN representing the number of elements in the sorted array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the elements of the sorted array.
The third line contains the integer XXX to be inserted into the array.


Output Format
Print the elements of the modified sorted array, separated by spaces, after inserting XXX into the array.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N, the size of the sorted array.
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers in non‑decreasing order.
- The third line contains the integer X to be inserted.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the elements of the array after inserting X, separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. After inserting 4 into the sorted array, the array becomes 1 3 4 5 7 9.
The value 11 is larger than all existing elements, so it is placed at the end.
The target 2 is equal to an existing element; it is inserted right after the first 2, preserving non‑decreasing order.

hidden.label: general case 2 - equal to smallest element

solution.approach: For each test case we have a non‑decreasing array. Starting from the last element we shift elements that are larger than X one position to the right. The first position where the element is not larger than X is the correct insertion point. This is a classic linear‑time insertion using the two‑pointer technique. The algorithm runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (the array is modified in‑place).

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        n = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(n)]
        x = int(next(it))
        i = n - 1
        while i >= 0 and arr[i] > x:
            i -= 1
        arr.insert(i + 1, x)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, arr)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
